Alert.description: You add your Proficiency Bonus to Initiative rolls. You can't be Surprised while conscious. Other creatures don't gain Advantage on attack rolls against you as a result of being unseen by you.

Crafter.description: You gain proficiency with three Artisan's Tools of your choice. Items you craft with tools cost 20% less, and you can craft items during a Long Rest.

Healer.description: You gain proficiency with Healer's Kit. When you use it to stabilize a dying creature, that creature regains 1 Hit Point. As an action, you can use a charge to restore 2d6 + your Proficiency Bonus Hit Points to a creature.

Lucky.description: You have a number of Luck Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus. Before or after you roll a d20 for a d20 Test, you can spend a Luck Point to give yourself Advantage on that roll.

Musician.description: You gain proficiency with three Musical Instruments of your choice. After finishing a Short or Long Rest, you and allies who hear your performance gain Heroic Inspiration.

Savage Attacker.description: You've trained to deal particularly damaging strikes. Once per turn when you hit a target with a weapon, you can roll the damage dice twice and use either roll against the target.

Skilled.description: You gain proficiency in any combination of three skills or tools of your choice.

Tavern Brawler.description: You deal 1d4 enhanced Unarmed Strike damage. When you hit with your Unarmed Strike as part of the Attack action, you can make one Unarmed Strike as a Bonus Action. You have proficiency with improvised weapons.

Tough.description: Your Hit Point maximum increases by an amount equal to twice your level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a level thereafter, your Hit Point maximum increases by an additional 2 Hit Points.

Ability Score Improvement.description: Increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. This feat can't increase an ability score above 20. Instead, you can choose another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

Actor.description: Gain proficiency in the Performance and Deception skills (or Expertise if already proficient). You can mimic another person's voice and mannerisms; a successful DC 15 Insight check is needed to detect the mimicry.

Athlete.description: Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20). Standing up from Prone costs only 5 feet of movement. Climbing doesn't cost extra movement. You can make a running Long or High Jump after moving only 5 feet.

Charger.description: Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20). When you take the Dash action, you can make one melee attack or shove as a Bonus Action; if you moved at least 10 feet in a straight line toward the target, the attack deals +1d8 damage or the shove pushes 10 feet.

Chef.description: Gain proficiency with Cook's Utensils (or Expertise if proficient). After a Long Rest, you can cook a number of treats equal to your Proficiency Bonus that each restore 1d8 HP when consumed. During a Short Rest, you can prepare a meal that grants temporary HP equal to your Proficiency Bonus to up to five creatures.

Crossbow Expert.description: Increase DEX by 1 (max 20). Ignore the loading property of crossbows. Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose disadvantage on ranged attacks. When you attack with a one-handed weapon, you can use a Bonus Action to attack with a hand crossbow.

Crusher.description: Increase STR or CON by 1 (max 20). Once per turn when you deal bludgeoning damage, you can push the target 5 feet. When you score a critical hit with bludgeoning damage, all attack rolls against the target have advantage until the start of your next turn.

Defensive Duelist.description: Increase DEX by 1 (max 20). When you are hit by a melee attack while holding a finesse weapon, you can use a Reaction to add your Proficiency Bonus to your AC for that attack, potentially turning the hit into a miss.

Dual Wielder.description: Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20). When you make an extra attack with your off-hand weapon via Two-Weapon Fighting, you can add your ability modifier to the damage. You can use two-weapon fighting even if both weapons aren't Light. You can draw or stow two one-handed weapons at once.

Durable.description: Increase CON by 1 (max 20). When you roll a Hit Die to recover HP during a Short Rest, you can reroll it and use either result. You can use a Bonus Action to regain HP equal to 1d4 + CON modifier a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining uses on a Long Rest.

Elemental Adept.description: Choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. Spells and magical effects you create ignore resistance to that damage type. When you roll damage of that type, treat any 1 on a damage die as a 2.

Fey Touched.description: Increase INT, WIS, or CHA by 1 (max 20). You learn the Misty Step spell and one level 1 divination or enchantment spell of your choice. You can cast each once per Long Rest without a spell slot, or using spell slots you have.

Grappler.description: Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20). You have advantage on attack rolls against creatures you are grappling. When you grapple a creature, you can also deal damage equal to your unarmed strike. Creatures at least one size smaller than you can be grappled and restrained simultaneously.

Great Weapon Master.description: Increase STR by 1 (max 20). When you score a critical hit or reduce a creature to 0 HP with a melee weapon, you can make one melee weapon attack as a Bonus Action. Heavy weapon attacks you make deal +1d6 damage on a hit.

Heavily Armored.description: Gain proficiency with Heavy Armor. Increase STR by 1 (max 20).

Heavy Armor Master.description: Increase STR by 1 (max 20). While wearing Heavy Armor, bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage you take from nonmagical weapons is reduced by 3.

Inspiring Leader.description: Increase WIS or CHA by 1 (max 20). As a Magic action, you can spend 10 minutes giving an inspiring speech to up to 6 willing allies (including yourself) who can hear and understand you. Each gains temporary HP equal to your level + your Charisma modifier.

Keen Mind.description: Increase INT by 1 (max 20). You always know which direction is north and how many hours until sunrise/sunset. You can accurately recall anything you have seen or heard in the past month. You have advantage on saving throws to maintain Concentration.

Lightly Armored.description: Gain proficiency with Light Armor and Shields. Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20).

Mage Slayer.description: Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20). When a creature within 5 feet of you casts a spell, you can use a Reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it. When you damage a creature that is concentrating on a spell, it has disadvantage on the save to maintain Concentration.

Martial Weapon Training.description: Gain proficiency with all Martial weapons. Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20).

Medium Armor Master.description: Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20). While wearing Medium Armor, you can add up to +3 (instead of +2) from DEX to your AC. Wearing Medium Armor doesn't impose disadvantage on Stealth checks.

Moderately Armored.description: Gain proficiency with Medium Armor and Shields. Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20).

Mounted Combatant.description: Increase STR, DEX, or WIS by 1 (max 20). While mounted, you have advantage on melee attack rolls against unmounted creatures smaller than your mount. You can force an attack that targets your mount to target you instead. If your mount would take damage, you can use a Reaction to have it take no damage (Evasion-style) on a successful DEX save.

Observant.description: Increase INT or WIS by 1 (max 20). You can read lips if you can see a creature's mouth and know its language. You have a +5 bonus to your passive Perception and passive Investigation scores.

Piercer.description: Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20). Once per turn when you deal piercing damage, you can reroll one of the damage dice; you must use the new result. When you score a critical hit that deals piercing damage, you roll one additional piercing damage die.

Poisoner.description: Gain proficiency with the Poisoner's Kit. Coating a weapon with poison you create takes only a Bonus Action. You can create a number of doses of potent poison per Long Rest equal to your Proficiency Bonus; it deals 2d8 poison damage on a failed CON save (DC 8 + PB + INT).

Polearm Master.description: Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20). When you take the Attack action with a glaive, halberd, pike, quarterstaff, or spear, you can use a Bonus Action to make a melee attack with the opposite end dealing 1d4 bludgeoning damage. Creatures provoke Opportunity Attacks when they enter your reach.

Resilient.description: Choose one ability score. Gain proficiency in saving throws using that ability. Increase that ability score by 1 (max 20).

Ritual Caster.description: Increase INT, WIS, or CHA by 1 (max 20). You have a ritual book containing two level 1 ritual spells of your choice (from any class list). You can cast them as rituals. You can add new ritual spells found in the world. Spellcasting ability is the increased ability score.

Sentinel.description: Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20). Creatures you hit with an Opportunity Attack have their speed reduced to 0. Creatures within your reach provoke Opportunity Attacks even if they took the Disengage action. You can use a Reaction to make a melee attack against a creature that attacks a creature other than you.

Shadow Touched.description: Increase INT, WIS, or CHA by 1 (max 20). You learn the Invisibility spell and one level 1 illusion or necromancy spell of your choice. You can cast each once per Long Rest without a spell slot, or using spell slots you have.

Sharpshooter.description: Increase DEX by 1 (max 20). Ranged weapon attacks ignore half cover and three-quarters cover. Attacking at long range doesn't impose disadvantage. Ranged weapon attacks you make deal +1d8 damage on a hit.

Shield Master.description: Increase STR by 1 (max 20). If you take the Attack action, you can use a Bonus Action to try to shove a creature within 5 feet. If you are subjected to an effect that allows a DEX save for half damage, you can use a Reaction to take no damage on a success (Evasion style). You add your shield's AC bonus to DEX saves while wielding it.

Skill Expert.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 20). Gain proficiency in one skill. Gain Expertise (double proficiency) in one skill you are proficient in.

Skulker.description: Increase DEX by 1 (max 20). You can try to hide when lightly obscured. While hidden, missing a ranged attack doesn't reveal your position. Dim light doesn't impose disadvantage on your Perception checks.

Slasher.description: Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 20). Once per turn when you deal slashing damage, you can reduce the target's speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. When you score a critical hit that deals slashing damage, the target has disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of your next turn.

Speedy.description: Increase DEX or CON by 1 (max 20). Your Speed increases by 10 feet. When you take the Dash action, Difficult Terrain doesn't cost extra movement for the rest of the turn. You can take the Dash or Disengage action as a Bonus Action.

Telekinetic.description: Increase INT, WIS, or CHA by 1 (max 20). You learn the Mage Hand cantrip or can cast it without visible components. As a Bonus Action, you can use telekinesis to push a creature within 30 feet up to 5 feet away (STR save DC 8 + PB + spellcasting ability to resist).

War Caster.description: Gain advantage on CON saving throws to maintain Concentration on a spell. You can perform the somatic components of a spell while holding weapons or a shield. When a creature provokes an Opportunity Attack from you, you can use your Reaction to cast a spell at it (1 action or Reaction spell, single target).

Archery.description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.

Blessed Warrior.description: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Cleric spell list. WIS is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another from the Cleric list.

Blind Fighting.description: You have Blindsight with a range of 10 feet. Within that range, you can see invisible creatures and obstacles even if you are Blinded or in magical Darkness.

Defense.description: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

Druidic Warrior.description: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Druid spell list. WIS is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another from the Druid list.

Dueling.description: When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

Great Weapon Fighting.description: When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack with a two-handed or versatile weapon held with two hands, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

Interception.description: When a creature you can see hits a target other than you within 5 feet of you with an attack, you can use a Reaction to reduce the damage to the target by 1d10 + your Proficiency Bonus (minimum 0). You must be holding a shield or a simple or martial weapon.

Protection.description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can use a Reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll. You must be wielding a shield.

Thrown Weapon Fighting.description: You can draw a weapon that has the Thrown property as part of the attack you make with it. In addition, when you hit with a thrown weapon, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.

Two-Weapon Fighting.description: When you make the extra attack granted by Two-Weapon Fighting, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack.

Unarmed Fighting.description: Your Unarmed Strike damage die is 1d6 (or 1d8 if you have both hands free). At the start of each of your turns, you can deal 1d4 bludgeoning damage to one creature you are grappling.

Boon of Combat Prowess.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 30). When you miss with a melee attack, you can hit instead — usable once per Short or Long Rest.

Boon of Dimensional Travel.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 30). As a Bonus Action, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can use this a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining uses on a Long Rest.

Boon of Energy Resistance.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 30). Choose one damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Necrotic, Poison, Psychic, Radiant, or Thunder. You gain resistance to that type and can change your choice when you finish a Long Rest.

Boon of Fate.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 30). When another creature within 60 feet makes a d20 Test, you can use a Reaction to roll 2d4 and apply the result as a bonus or penalty to the roll. You have a number of uses equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regained on a Long Rest.

Boon of Fortitude.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 30). Your HP maximum increases by 40.

Boon of Irresistible Offense.description: Increase STR or DEX by 1 (max 30). Your melee and thrown weapon attacks and Unarmed Strikes deal an extra +1d8 damage. Attacks you make ignore resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

Boon of Recovery.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 30). You can use a Bonus Action to spend Hit Dice from a pool of 10d10 to regain HP (regained on Long Rest). When you would die from damage while at 0 HP, you can choose to drop to 1 HP instead (once per Long Rest).

Boon of Skill.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 30). Gain proficiency in all skills. Choose one skill to gain Expertise in.

Boon of Speed.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 30). Your Speed increases by 30 feet. As a Bonus Action, you can take the Dash or Disengage action, and Opportunity Attacks against you have disadvantage for the rest of the turn.

Boon of Spell Recall.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 30). You can cast any spell you know or have prepared without expending a spell slot. Once you do so, you cannot use this benefit again until you finish a Long Rest.

Boon of the Night Spirit.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 30). While in dim light or darkness you are Invisible. You also gain resistance to necrotic damage and can move through creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain (taking 1d10 force damage if you end your turn inside an object).

Boon of Truesight.description: Increase one ability score by 1 (max 30). You gain Truesight with a range of 60 feet, allowing you to see through magical darkness, invisible creatures, illusions, shapechangers, and into the Ethereal Plane.

Spell Sniper.description: Spell attack rolls ignore Half and Three-Quarters Cover. No Disadvantage on spell attacks within 5 ft. Spells with 10+ ft range that require attack rolls gain +60 ft range. +1 INT/WIS/CHA.

Telepathic.description: Telepathically speak to creatures within 60 ft (they understand you even without shared language). Cast Detect Thoughts 1/Long Rest without a slot. +1 INT/WIS/CHA.

Weapon Master.description: Proficiency with all Martial weapons. +1 STR/DEX.

Spellcasting (Artificer).description: You have learned how to channel magical energy through objects. See the Player's Handbook for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Artificer spells, which appear in the Artificer spell list later in the class's description. Tools Required: You produce your Artificer spells through tools.

Tinker's Magic.description: You know the Mending cantrip. As a Magic action while holding Tinker's Tools, you can create one item in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of yourself, choosing the item from the following list: Ball Bearings, Basket, Bedroll, Bell, Blanket, Block and Tackle, Glass Bottle, Bucket, Caltrops, Candle, Crowbar, Flask, Grappling Hook, Hunting Trap, Jug, Lamp, Manacles, Net, Oil, Paper, Parchment, Pole, Pouch, Rope, Sack, Shovel, String, Tinderbox, Torch, Vial. The item vanishes after 1 hour or when you use this feature again.

Tinker's Magic.spell.Mending.description: This spell repairs a single break or tear in an object you touch, such as a broken chain link, two halves of a broken key, a torn cloak, or a leaking wineskin. As long as the break or tear is no larger than 1 foot in any dimension, you mend it, leaving no trace of the former damage. This spell can physically repair a magic item, but it can't restore magic to such an object.

Replicate Magic Item.description: You have learned arcane plans that you use to make magic items. Plans Known: When you gain this feature, choose four plans to learn from the Magic Item Plans (Artificer Level 2+) table (see the Dungeon Master's Guide for the items' descriptions). Bag of Holding, Cap of Water Breathing, Sending Stones, and +1 Wand of the War Mage are recommended.

Replicate Magic Item.field.Plans Known.description: When you gain this feature, choose four plans to learn from the Magic Item Plans (Artificer Level 2+) table (see the Dungeon Master's Guide for the items' descriptions). Bag of Holding, Cap of Water Breathing, Sending Stones, and +1 Wand of the War Mage are recommended.

Replicate Magic Item.field.Plans Known.o.description: This ceramic jug appears to be able to hold a gallon of liquid and weighs 12 pounds whether full or empty. Sloshing sounds can be heard from within the jug when it is shaken. As a Magic action, you can command the jug to produce a chosen liquid. Each day you can produce one of: acid (1 flask), basic poison (1 vial), beer (4 gallons), honey (1 gallon), mayonnaise (2 gallons), oil (1 quart), vinegar (2 gallons), water (8 gallons), or wine (1 gallon).

This bag has an interior space considerably larger than its outside dimensions (roughly 2 feet in diameter at the mouth and 4 feet deep). The bag can hold up to 500 pounds, not exceeding a volume of 64 cubic feet. The bag weighs 15 pounds, regardless of its contents. Retrieving an item from the bag requires an action.

While wearing this cap underwater, you can speak its command word as a Magic action to create a bubble of air around your head. The bubble allows you to breathe normally underwater. The bubble lasts until you speak the command word again or until you are no longer underwater.
While wearing these dark lenses, you have Darkvision out to 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, wearing the goggles increases its range by 60 feet.

The tool takes the form of a wrench, a screwdriver, or another basic tool. As a Magic action, you can touch it and transform it into a type of Artisan's Tools of your choice. Whatever form the tool takes, you have proficiency with it when you use it.

This magic weapon automatically creates its own ammunition, producing one piece of nonmagical ammunition each time you make a ranged attack with it. The ammunition created by the weapon vanishes the instant after it hits or misses a target.
This magic weapon returns to the wielder's hand immediately after it is used to make a ranged attack. The weapon magically flies back to your hand after the attack, whether it hits or misses.

This 60-foot length of silk rope weighs 3 pounds and can hold up to 3,000 pounds. If you hold one end of the rope and use a Magic action to speak its command word, the rope animates. You can then use a Bonus Action to direct the other end to move toward a destination of your choice. The rope moves at 10 feet per round.

Sending stones come in pairs, with each smooth stone carved to match its counterpart. While you touch one stone, you can use a Magic action to cast Sending from it. The spell can only be cast targeting the bearer of the other stone. At dawn, the sending property of each stone recharges.
While holding this shield, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.
This wand has 3 charges. While holding it, you can expend 1 charge as a Magic action to cast Detect Magic from it. The wand regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

This wand has 3 charges. While holding it, you can expend 1 charge as a Magic action. If a secret door or trap is within 30 feet of you, the wand pulses and points at the nearest one. The wand regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.
While holding this wand, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls. In addition, you ignore Half Cover when making a spell attack. You must be a spellcaster to attune to this item.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

While wearing these wraps, your unarmed strikes are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming immunity and resistance to nonmagical attacks. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with unarmed strikes, which deal your choice of Force damage or their normal damage type.
You gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class while wearing this armor.
While you wear these boots, your steps make no sound, regardless of the surface you are moving across. You also have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks that rely on moving silently.
While wearing these boots, you can take a Bonus Action to teleport up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You must have occupied that space at some point during the current turn.

While you wear this cloak with its hood up, Wisdom (Perception) checks made to see you have Disadvantage, and you have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide, as the cloak's color shifts to camouflage you. Pulling the hood up or down requires a Magic action.
While wearing this cloak with its hood up, you can breathe underwater, and you have a Swim Speed of 60 feet. Pulling the hood up or down requires a Magic action.

This magic weapon grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. While holding it, you can use a Bonus Action to cause it to shed Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet (or extinguish as a Bonus Action). The weapon has 4 charges. You can use a Reaction immediately after being hit by an attack to expend 1 charge and force the attacker to make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the attacker has the Blinded condition until the end of its next turn. The weapon regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.

These crystal lenses fit over the eyes. They have 3 charges. While wearing them, you can expend 1 charge as a Magic action to cast Charm Person (save DC 13) on a humanoid within 30 feet of you. The lenses regain all expended charges daily at dawn.

These crystal lenses fit over the eyes. While wearing them, you can see much better than normal out to a range of 1 foot. You have Advantage on Intelligence (Investigation) checks that rely on sight when examining an object or area within 1 foot of you.
These gloves are invisible while worn. While wearing them, you gain a +5 bonus to Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) checks and Dexterity checks made to pick locks.
While wearing this helm, you have Advantage on Initiative rolls. In addition, you can't be surprised, provided you aren't Incapacitated.

While lit, this hooded lantern burns for 6 hours on 1 pint of oil, shedding bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Invisible creatures and objects are visible while in the bright light shed by the lantern.

This item can send an invigorating jolt through the wearer's nervous system. It has 4 charges. When you fail a Constitution saving throw to maintain Concentration on a spell, you can take a Reaction and expend 1 charge to succeed on the saving throw instead. The item regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.

While wearing this necklace, you can breathe normally in any environment, and you have Advantage on saving throws made against harmful gases and vapors (such as those created by Cloudkill and Stinking Cloud spells).

You must be proficient with wind instruments to use these pipes. They have 3 charges. You can use a Magic action to play them and expend 1 charge to create an eerie, haunting melody. Each creature within 30 feet of you that hears you play must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or become Frightened of you for 1 minute. The pipes regain 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

While holding this shield, you gain a +1 bonus to AC in addition to the shield's normal bonus. The shield has 4 charges. When you are hit by a melee attack while holding it, you can use a Reaction immediately after being hit to expend 1 charge and push the attacker up to 15 feet away from you. The shield regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.
You have a Swim Speed of 40 feet while wearing this ring.
While wearing this ring, you can stand on and move across any liquid surface as if it were solid ground.
While holding this shield, you have Advantage on Initiative rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks. The shield is emblazoned with a symbol of an eye.
While wearing this ring, you can use a Magic action to recover one expended spell slot of 3rd level or lower. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

This wand has 7 charges. While holding it, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast Magic Missile from it. For 1 charge, you cast the 1st-level version of the spell. You can increase the spell slot level by one for each additional charge you expend. The wand regains 1d6+1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

This wand has 7 charges. While holding it, you can expend 1 charge as a Magic action to cast Web from it (save DC 15). The wand regains 1d6+1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

This magic weapon warns you of danger. While the weapon is on your person, you have Advantage on Initiative rolls. In addition, you and any of your companions within 30 feet of you can't be surprised, except when Incapacitated by something other than nonmagical sleep. The weapon magically awakens you and your companions within 30 feet if any of you are sleeping naturally when combat begins.

You have Resistance to one type of damage while wearing this armor. The GM chooses the type or determines it randomly from among the following: Acid, Cold, Fire, Force, Lightning, Necrotic, Poison, Psychic, Radiant, Thunder.

While you wear these boots, your walking speed becomes 30 feet (unless your walking speed is higher), and your speed isn't reduced by carrying a Heavy load or wearing Heavy Armor. In addition, you can jump three times the normal distance, though you can't jump farther than your remaining movement would allow.

These furred boots are snug and warm. While wearing them, you can tolerate temperatures as low as −50 degrees Fahrenheit. You also have Resistance to Cold damage and ignore Difficult Terrain created by ice or snow.
While wearing these bracers, you have proficiency with the Longbow and Shortbow, and you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls on ranged attacks made with such weapons.
While wearing this brooch, you have Resistance to Force damage, and you have Immunity to damage from the Magic Missile spell.
You gain a +1 bonus to AC and saving throws while you wear this cloak.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You can use a Bonus Action to cause thick, black poison to coat the blade. The poison remains for 1 minute or until an attack using this weapon hits a creature. That creature must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or take 2d10 Poison damage and have the Poisoned condition for 1 minute.
You gain a +1 bonus to AC while you wear this armor. You are considered proficient with this armor even if you lack proficiency with Medium armor.

These crystal lenses fit over the eyes. While wearing them, you have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight. In conditions of clear visibility, you can make out details of even extremely distant creatures and objects as small as 2 feet across.
Your Strength score is 19 while you wear these gauntlets. They have no effect on you if your Strength is already 19 or higher.

These gloves seem to almost reach out to catch flying missiles. When a ranged weapon attack hits you while you're wearing them, you can use your Reaction to reduce the damage by 1d10 + your Dexterity modifier, as long as your hand is free. If you reduce the damage to 0, you can catch the missile if it's small enough for you to hold in that hand.
While wearing these gloves, climbing and swimming don't cost you extra movement, and you gain a +5 bonus to Strength (Athletics) checks made to climb or swim.
While wearing this hat, you can cast Disguise Self from it at will as a Magic action. The spell ends if the hat is removed.
Your Intelligence score is 19 while you wear this headband. It has no effect on you if your Intelligence is already 19 or higher.

While wearing this helm, you can cast Detect Thoughts (save DC 13) as a Magic action. While maintaining Concentration on the spell, you can use a Bonus Action to send a telepathic message to a creature you are focused on. It can reply as a Bonus Action.
The medallion has 3 charges. While wearing it, you can use a Magic action and expend 1 charge to cast Detect Thoughts (save DC 13) from it. The medallion regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

While you wear this pendant, you stabilize whenever you are Dying at the start of your turn. In addition, whenever you roll a Hit Die to regain Hit Points, double the number of Hit Points it restores.

You must be proficient with wind instruments to use these pipes. While you play them, you can use a Magic action to call rats within half a mile of you, provided no bright sunlight is present. You can call a swarm of rats (if available). The swarm moves toward the music by the most direct route available. Pipes of the Sewers also have 3 charges; you can expend charges to cast Animal Friendship (on rats and giant rats only).

Each of the quiver's three compartments connects to an extradimensional space that allows the quiver to hold numerous items while never weighing more than 2 pounds. The shortest compartment holds up to 60 Arrows or Bolts. The midsize compartment holds up to 18 Javelins or similar items. The longest compartment holds up to 6 long objects, such as bows, Quarterstaffs, or Spears.
When you fall while wearing this ring, you descend 60 feet per round and take no damage from falling.
While wearing this ring, you can cast Jump as a Bonus Action from it, but can target only yourself. The spell ends at the end of your turn if you haven't already landed.

While wearing this ring, you are immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, know your alignment, or know your creature type. Creatures can telepathically communicate with you only if you allow it. If you die while wearing the ring, your soul enters it, unless it already houses a soul.
While holding this shield, you gain a +2 bonus to Armor Class in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.

While you wear these light shoes, you have a Climb Speed equal to your Speed. The slippers allow you to move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving your hands free.

When you attune to these metallic lungs, they replace the lungs in your chest (which disappear). They allow you to breathe normally in any environment (including a vacuum) and even in an antimagic field, as this function can't be suppressed by magic. You have Advantage on saving throws against harmful gases (e.g. Cloudkill, Stinking Cloud, inhaled poisons, gaseous breath weapons). As a Magic action, you can exhale a gust of wind as if casting Gust of Wind (save DC 15) without components. This property resets at dawn.
While holding this wand, you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls. In addition, you ignore Half Cover when making a spell attack. You must be a spellcaster to attune to this item.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

While you wear these boots, you have a Fly Speed equal to your walking speed. You can use the boots to fly for up to 4 hours, all at once or in several shorter flights, each one using a minimum of 1 minute from the duration. If you are flying when the duration expires, you descend at a rate of 30 feet per round until you land. The boots regain 2 hours of flying capability for every 12 hours they aren't in use.

While wearing these wraps, your unarmed strikes are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming immunity and resistance to nonmagical attacks. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with unarmed strikes, which deal your choice of Force damage or their normal damage type.
Your Constitution score is 19 while you wear this amulet. It has no effect on you if your Constitution is already 19 or higher.

This prosthetic appendage attaches to your arm (at the wrist, elbow, or shoulder). It is a fully capable magic melee weapon with which you're proficient, dealing 1d8 Force damage on a hit. It also has the Thrown property with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. When thrown, the arm detaches and flies to hit the target, then immediately returns and reattaches. You can use a Magic action to remove the prosthetic.
You gain a +2 bonus to Armor Class while wearing this armor.

You gain a +2 bonus to AC against ranged attacks while you wield this shield. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC. Whenever an attacker makes a ranged attack against a target within 5 feet of you, you can use your Reaction to become the target of the attack instead.
While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 21. The belt has no effect on you if your Strength is already equal to or greater than 21.
While you wear these boots, you can cast Levitate on yourself at will as a Magic action.

While you wear these boots, you can use a Bonus Action to click the boots' heels together. If you do, the boots double your walking speed, and any creature that makes an Opportunity Attack against you has Disadvantage on the attack roll. The effect ends when you click your heels together again, or when you have been flying for a total of 10 minutes. The boots can't be used this way again until you finish a Long Rest.
While wearing these bracers, you gain a +2 bonus to AC if you are wearing no armor and using no shield.

While wearing this cloak, you have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. In an area of dim light or darkness, you can grip the edges of the cloak with both hands and use it to fly at a speed of 40 feet. If you ever fail to grip the cloak's edges while flying in this way, or if you are no longer in dim light or darkness, you lose this flying speed. While in darkness, you can cast Polymorph on yourself as a Magic action, transforming into a bat.

You can use a Magic action to place these shackles on an Incapacitated creature. The shackles adjust to fit a creature of Small to Large size. In addition to serving as mundane manacles, the shackles prevent a creature bound by them from using any method of extradimensional movement, including teleportation or travel to a different plane of existence.

You can use a Bonus Action to speak this magic sword's command word, causing flames to erupt from the blade. These flames shed bright light in a 40-foot radius and dim light for an additional 40 feet. While the sword is ablaze, it deals an extra 2d6 Fire damage to any target it hits. The flames last until you use a Bonus Action to speak the command word again or until you drop or sheathe the sword.

This gem has 3 charges. As a Magic action, you can speak the gem's command word and expend 1 charge. For the next 10 minutes, you have Truesight out to 120 feet when you peer through the gem. The gem regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

You can use a Magic action to speak the horn's command word and then blow the horn, which emits a thunderous blast in a 30-foot Cone. Each creature in the Cone must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 5d6 Thunder damage and has the Deafened condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only. Glass and crystal objects that aren't being worn or carried take 5d6 Thunder damage from the blast. Each use of the horn's magic has a 20 percent chance of causing the horn to explode. The explosion deals 10d6 Fire damage to you and destroys the horn.
While you wear this ring, Difficult Terrain doesn't cost you extra movement. In addition, magic can neither reduce your Speed nor cause you to be Paralyzed or Restrained.
You gain a +1 bonus to AC and saving throws while wearing this ring.

This ring has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the ring, you can use a Magic action to expend 1 to 3 of its charges to make a ranged spell attack against one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The ring produces a spectral ram's head and makes its attack with a +7 bonus. On a hit, for each charge you spend, the target takes 2d10 Force damage and is pushed 5 feet away from you.

Artificer Subclass.description: You gain an Artificer subclass of your choice. The Alchemist, Armorer, Artillerist, and Battle Smith, and Cartographer subclasses are detailed after this class's description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Artificer levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Artificer level or lower.

Magic Item Tinker.description: Your Replicate Magic Item feature gains the following options. Charge Magic Item: As a Bonus Action, you can touch a magic item within 5 feet of yourself that you created with Replicate Magic Item and that uses charges. You expend a level 1+ spell slot and recharge the item. The number of charges the item regains is equal to the level of spell slot expended.

Flash of Genius.description: When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you fails an ability check or a saving throw, you can take a Reaction to add a bonus to the roll, potentially causing it to succeed. The bonus equals your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1). You can take this Reaction a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once).

Magic Item Adept.description: You can now attune to up to four magic items at once.

Spell-Storing Item.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can touch one Simple or Martial weapon or one item that you can use as a Spellcasting Focus, and you store a spell in it, choosing a level 1, 2, or 3 Artificer spell that has a casting time of an action and doesn't require a Material component that is consumed by the spell (you needn't have the spell prepared).

Advanced Artifice.description: You gain the following benefits. Magic Item Savant: You can now attune to up to five magic items at once. Refreshed Genius: When you finish a Short Rest, you regain one expended use of your Flash of Genius feature.

Magic Item Master.description: You can now attune to up to six magic items at once.

Epic Boon.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Spell Recall is recommended.

Soul of Artifice.description: You have developed a mystical connection to your magic items, which you can draw on for aid. You gain the following benefits. Cheat Death: If you're reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, you can disintegrate any number of Uncommon or Rare magic items created by your Replicate Magic Item feature.

Alchemist Spells.description: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Alchemist Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

Alchemist Spells.spell.Healing Word.description: A creature of your choice that you can see within range regains Hit Points equal to 2d4 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Alchemist Spells.spell.Ray of Sickness.description: You shoot a greenish ray at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 2d8 Poison damage and has the Poisoned condition until the end of your next turn.

Alchemist Spells.spell.Flaming Sphere.description: You create a 5-foot-diameter sphere of fire in an unoccupied space on the ground within range. It lasts for the duration. Any creature that ends its turn within 5 feet of the sphere makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d6 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

Alchemist Spells.spell.Melf's Acid Arrow.description: A shimmering green arrow streaks toward a target within range and bursts in a spray of acid. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 4d4 Acid damage and 2d4 Acid damage at the end of its next turn.

Alchemist Spells.spell.Gaseous Form.description: A willing creature you touch shape-shifts, along with everything it's wearing and carrying, into a misty cloud for the duration. The spell ends on the target if it drops to 0 Hit Points or if it takes a Magic action to end the spell on itself.

Alchemist Spells.spell.Mass Healing Word.description: Up to six creatures of your choice that you can see within range regain Hit Points equal to 2d4 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Alchemist Spells.spell.Death Ward.description: You touch a creature and grant it a measure of protection from death. The first time the target would drop to 0 Hit Points before the spell ends, the target instead drops to 1 Hit Points, and the spell ends.

Alchemist Spells.spell.Vitriolic Sphere.description: You point at a location within range, and a glowing, 1-foot-diameter ball of acid streaks there and explodes in a 20-foot-radius Sphere [Area of Effect]. Each creature in that area makes a Dexterity saving throw.

Alchemist Spells.spell.Cloudkill.description: You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere [Area of Effect] of yellow-green fog centered on a point within range. The fog lasts for the duration or until strong wind (such as the one created by Gust of Wind) disperses it, ending the spell. Its area is Heavily Obscured.

Alchemist Spells.spell.Raise Dead.description: With a touch, you revive a dead creature if it has been dead no longer than 10 days and it wasn't Undead when it died. The creature returns to life with 1 Hit Points. This spell also neutralizes any poisons that affected the creature at the time of death.

Experimental Elixir.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest while holding Alchemist's Supplies, you can use that tool to magically produce two elixirs. For each elixir, roll on the Experimental Elixir table for the elixir's effect, which is triggered when someone drinks the elixir. The elixir appears in a vial, and the vial vanishes when the elixir is drunk or poured out.

Experimental Elixir.field.Elixirs.option.description: The drinker regains a number of Hit Points equal to 2d4 + your Intelligence modifier.
The drinker's Speed increases by 10 feet for 1 hour.
The drinker gains a +1 bonus to AC for 10 minutes.
The drinker can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to every attack roll and saving throw they make for 1 minute.
The drinker gains a Fly Speed of 10 feet for 10 minutes.

Tools of the Trade.description: You gain the following benefits. Tool Proficiency: You gain proficiency with Calligrapher's Supplies and Cartographer's Tools. If you already have one of these proficiencies, you gain proficiency with one other type of Artisan's Tools of your choice (or with two other types if you have both).

Alchemical Savant.description: Whenever you cast a spell using your Alchemist's Supplies as the Spellcasting Focus, you gain a bonus to one roll of the spell. That roll must restore Hit Points or be a damage roll that deals Acid, Fire, or Poison damage. The bonus equals your Intelligence modifier (minimum bonus of +1).

Restorative Reagents.description: You can cast Lesser Restoration without expending a spell slot and without preparing the spell, provided you use Alchemist's Supplies as the Spellcasting Focus. You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Chemical Mastery.description: You gain the following benefits. Alchemical Eruption: When you cast an Artificer spell that deals Acid, Fire, or Poison damage to a target, you can also deal 2d8 Force damage to that target. You can use this benefit only once on each of your turns. Chemical Resistance: You gain Resistance to Acid damage and Poison damage. You also gain Immunity to the Poisoned condition.

Arcane Armor.description: As a Magic action while you have Smith's Tools in hand, you can turn a suit of armor you are wearing into Arcane Armor. The armor continues to be Arcane Armor until you don another suit of armor or you die. You gain the following benefits while wearing your Arcane Armor. No Strength Requirement: If the armor normally has a Strength requirement, the Arcane Armor lacks this requirement for you.

Armor Model.description: You can customize your Arcane Armor. When you do so, choose one of the following armor models: Dreadnaught, Guardian, or Infiltrator. The model you choose gives you special benefits while you wear it. Each model includes a special weapon. When you attack with that weapon, you can add your Intelligence modifier, instead of your Strength or Dexterity modifier, to the attack and damage rolls.

Armorer Spells.description: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Armorer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

Armorer Spells.spell.Magic Missile.description: You create three glowing darts of magical force. Each dart strikes a creature of your choice that you can see within range. A dart deals 1d4 + 1 Force damage to its target. The darts all strike simultaneously, and you can direct them to hit one creature or several.

Armorer Spells.spell.Thunderwave.description: You unleash a wave of thunderous energy. Each creature in a 15-foot Cube [Area of Effect] originating from you makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d8 Thunder damage and is pushed 10 feet away from you.

Armorer Spells.spell.Mirror Image.description: Three illusory duplicates of yourself appear in your space. Until the spell ends, the duplicates move with you and mimic your actions, shifting position so it's impossible to track which image is real.

Armorer Spells.spell.Shatter.description: A loud noise erupts from a point of your choice within range. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere [Area of Effect] centered there makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 3d8 Thunder damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A Construct has Disadvantage on the save.

Armorer Spells.spell.Hypnotic Pattern.description: You create a twisting pattern of colors in a 30-foot Cube [Area of Effect] within range. The pattern appears for a moment and vanishes. Each creature in the area who can see the pattern must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition for the duration.

Armorer Spells.spell.Lightning Bolt.description: A stroke of lightning forming a 100-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line [Area of Effect] blasts out from you in a direction you choose. Each creature in the Line [Area of Effect] makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

Armorer Spells.spell.Fire Shield.description: Wispy flames wreathe your body for the duration, shedding Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet. The flames provide you with a warm shield or a chill shield, as you choose.

Armorer Spells.spell.Greater Invisibilit.description: A creature you touch has the Invisible condition until the spell ends. Anything the target is wearing or carrying is also Invisible. Unlike the Invisibility spell, this effect doesn't end if the target attacks, deals damage, or forces a saving throw.

Armorer Spells.spell.Passwall.description: A passage appears at a point that you can see on a wooden, plaster, or stone surface (such as a wall, ceiling, or floor) within range and lasts for the duration. You choose the opening's dimensions: up to 5 feet wide, 8 feet tall, and 20 feet deep.

Armorer Spells.spell.Wall of Force.description: An Invisible wall of force springs into existence at a point you choose within range. The wall appears in any orientation you choose, as a horizontal or vertical barrier or at an angle. It can be free floating or resting on a solid surface.

Dreadnaught.description: You design your armor to become a towering juggernaut in battle. It has the following features: Force Demolisher: An arcane wrecking ball or sledgehammer projects from your armor. The demolisher counts as a Simple Melee weapon with the R property, and it deals 1d10 Force damage on a hit.

Guardian.description: You design your armor to be in the front line of conflict. It has the following features:

Infiltrator.description: You customize your armor for subtler undertakings. It has the following features: Lightning Launcher: A gemlike node appears on your armor, from which you can shoot bolts of lightning. The launcher counts as a Simple Ranged weapon with a normal range of 90 feet and a long range of 300 feet, and it deals 1d6 Lightning damage on a hit.

Extra Attack.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. Moreover, you can cast one of your Wizard cantrips that has a casting time of an action in place of one of those attacks.

Improved Armorer.description: You gain the following benefits. Armor Replication: You learn an additional plan for your Replicate Magic Item feature, and it must be in the Armor category. If you replace that plan, you must replace it with another Armor plan. In addition, you can create an additional item with that feature, and the item must also be in the Armor category.

Perfected Armor.description: Your Arcane Armor gains additional benefits based on its model, as detailed below. Dreadnaught: The damage die of your Force Demolisher increases to 2d6 Force damage. In addition, when you use your Giant Stature, your reach increases by 10 feet, your size can increase to Large or Huge (your choice), and you have Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws for the duration.

Artillerist Spells.description: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Artillerist Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

Artillerist Spells.spell.Shield.description: An imperceptible barrier of magical force protects you. Until the start of your next turn, you have a +5 bonus to AC, including against the triggering attack, and you take no damage from Magic Missile.

Artillerist Spells.spell.Thunderwave.description: You unleash a wave of thunderous energy. Each creature in a 15-foot Cube [Area of Effect] originating from you makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d8 Thunder damage and is pushed 10 feet away from you.

Artillerist Spells.spell.Scorching Ray.description: You hurl three fiery rays. You can hurl them at one target within range or at several. Make a ranged spell attack for each ray. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 Fire damage.

Artillerist Spells.spell.Shatter.description: A loud noise erupts from a point of your choice within range. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere [Area of Effect] centered there makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 3d8 Thunder damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A Construct has Disadvantage on the save.

Artillerist Spells.spell.Fireball.description: A bright streak flashes from you to a point you choose within range and then blossoms with a low roar into a fiery explosion. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere [Area of Effect] centered on that point makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. The fire spreads around corners and ignites flammable objects that aren't being worn or carried. Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot: the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 3.

Artillerist Spells.spell.Wind Wall.description: A wall of strong wind rises from the ground at a point you choose within range. You can make the wall up to 50 feet long, 15 feet high, and 1 foot thick. You can shape the wall in any way you choose so long as it makes one continuous path along the ground. The wall lasts for the duration.

Artillerist Spells.spell.Ice Storm.description: Hail falls in a 20-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder [Area of Effect] centered on a point within range. Each creature in the Cylinder [Area of Effect] makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d8 Bludgeoning and 4d6 Cold damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The area becomes Difficult Terrain until the end of your next turn. Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot: the Bludgeoning damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4.

Artillerist Spells.spell.Wall of Fire.description: You create a wall of fire on a solid surface within range. You can make the wall up to 60 feet long, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick, or a ringed wall up to 20 feet in diameter, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick. The wall is opaque and lasts for the duration. When the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 5d8 Fire damage on a failed save, or half on a success. One side of the wall (your choice) deals 5d8 Fire damage to each creature that ends its turn within 10 feet of that side or enters the wall for the first time on a turn. Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot: the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4.

Artillerist Spells.spell.Cone of Cold.description: You unleash a blast of cold air. Each creature in a 60-foot Cone [Area of Effect] originating from you makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 8d8 Cold damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature killed by this spell becomes a frozen statue until it thaws.

Artillerist Spells.spell.Wall of Force.description: An Invisible wall of force springs into existence at a point you choose within range. The wall appears in any orientation you choose, as a horizontal or vertical barrier or at an angle. It can be free floating or resting on a solid surface.

Eldritch Cannon.description: Using Smith's Tools or Woodcarver's Tools, you can take a Magic action to create a Small or Tiny Eldritch Cannon in an unoccupied space on a horizontal surface within 5 feet of yourself. You determine its appearance, including whether you carry it or not (and your choice of legs or wheels, for the latter). It disappears if it is reduced to 0 Hit Points or after 1 hour.

Arcane Firearm.description: When you finish a Long Rest, you can use Woodcarver's Tools to carve special sigils into a Rod, Staff, Wand, or Martial Ranged weapon and thereby turn it into your Arcane Firearm. The sigils disappear from the object if you later carve them on a different item. The sigils otherwise last indefinitely. You can use your Arcane Firearm as a Spellcasting Focus for your Artificer spells.

Explosive Cannon.description: Every Eldritch Cannon you create is now more destructive. You gain the following benefits. Detonate: When your cannon takes damage, you can take a Reaction to command the cannon to detonate if you are within 60 feet of it.

Fortified Position.description: You're a master at forming well-defended emplacements using your Eldritch Cannon. You gain the following benefits. Shimmering Field Projection: You and your allies have Cover while within 10 feet of your Eldritch Cannon.

Battle Ready.description: Your combat training and your experiments with magic have paid off in two ways. Arcane Empowerment: When you attack with a magic weapon, you can use your Intelligence modifier, instead of your Strength or Dexterity modifier, for the attack and damage rolls. Weapon Knowledge: You gain proficiency with Martial weapons.

Battle Smith Spells.description: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Battle Smith Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

Battle Smith Spells.spell.Heroism.description: A willing creature you touch is imbued with bravery. Until the spell ends, the creature is immune to the Frightened condition and gains Temporary Hit Points equal to your spellcasting ability modifier at the start of each of its turns.

Battle Smith Spells.spell.Shield.description: An imperceptible barrier of magical force protects you. Until the start of your next turn, you have a +5 bonus to AC, including against the triggering attack, and you take no damage from Magic Missile.

Battle Smith Spells.spell.Shining Smite.description: The target hit by the strike takes an extra 2d6 Radiant damage from the attack. Until the spell ends, the target sheds Bright Light in a 5-foot radius, attack rolls against it have Advantage, and it can't benefit from the Invisible condition.

Battle Smith Spells.spell.Warding Bond.description: You touch another creature that is willing and create a mystic connection between you and the target until the spell ends. While the target is within 60 feet of you, it gains a +1 bonus to AC and saving throws, and it has Resistance to all damage.

Battle Smith Spells.spell.Aura of Vitali.description: An aura radiates from you in a 30-foot Emanation [Area of Effect] for the duration. When you create the aura and at the start of each of your turns while it persists, you can restore 2d6 Hit Points to one creature in it.

Battle Smith Spells.spell.Conjure Barrag.description: You brandish the weapon used to cast the spell and conjure similar spectral weapons (or ammunition appropriate to the weapon) that launch forward and then disappear. Each creature in a 60-foot Cone [Area of Effect] originating from you makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d8 damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The damage type is the same as the weapon's.

Battle Smith Spells.spell.Aura of Purity.description: An aura radiates from you in a 30-foot Emanation [Area of Effect] for the duration. While in the aura, you and your allies have Resistance to Poison damage and Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end effects that include the Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Frightened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Stunned conditions.

Battle Smith Spells.spell.Fire Shield.description: Wispy flames wreathe your body for the duration, shedding Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet. The flames provide you with a warm shield or a chill shield, as you choose.

Battle Smith Spells.spell.Banishing Smit.description: The target hit by the attack roll takes an extra 5d10 Force damage from the attack. If the attack reduces the target to 50 Hit Points or fewer, the target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be transported to a harmless demiplane for the duration.

Battle Smith Spells.spell.Mass Cure Woun.description: A wave of healing energy washes out from a point you can see within range. Choose up to six creatures in a 30-foot-radius Sphere [Area of Effect] centered on that point. Each target regains Hit Points equal to 5d8 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Steel Defender.description: Your tinkering has borne you a companion, a Steel Defender. You determine the defender's appearance and whether it has two legs or four; your choices don't affect the defender's game statistics. The Steel Defender is Friendly [Attitude] to you and your allies and obeys you. It vanishes if you die. The Defender in Combat: In combat, the defender acts during your turn.

Arcane Jolt.description: When either you hit a target with an attack roll using a magic weapon or your Steel Defender hits a target, you can channel magical energy through the strike to create one of the following effects: Destructive Energy: The target takes an extra 2d6 Force damage. Restorative Energy: Choose one creature or object you can see within 30 feet of the target.

Improved Defender.description: Your Arcane Jolt and Steel Defender have become more powerful, granting these benefits. Improved Jolt: The extra damage and healing of your Arcane Jolt both increase to 4d6. Improved Deflection: Whenever your Steel Defender uses its Deflect Attack, the attacker takes Force damage equal to 1d4 plus your Intelligence modifier.

Adventurer's Atlas.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest while holding Cartographer's Tools, you can use that tool to create a set of magical maps by touching at least two creatures (one of whom can be yourself), up to a maximum number of creatures equal to 1 plus your Intelligence modifier (minimum of two creatures).

Cartographer Spells.description: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Cartographer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

Cartographer Spells.spell.Faerie Fire.description: Objects in a 20-foot Cube [Area of Effect] within range are outlined in blue, green, or violet light (your choice). Each creature in the Cube [Area of Effect] is also outlined if it fails a Dexterity saving throw.

Cartographer Spells.spell.Guiding Bolt.description: You hurl a bolt of light toward a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, it takes 4d6 Radiant damage, and the next attack roll made against it before the end of your next turn has Advantage.

Cartographer Spells.spell.Healing Word.description: A creature of your choice that you can see within range regains Hit Points equal to 2d4 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Cartographer Spells.spell.Locate Object.description: Describe or name an object that is familiar to you. You sense the direction to the object's location if that object is within 1,000 feet of you. If the object is in motion, you know the direction of its movement.

Cartographer Spells.spell.Mind Spike.description: You drive a spike of psionic energy into the mind of one creature you can see within range. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw, taking 3d8 Psychic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

Cartographer Spells.spell.Call Lightning.description: A storm cloud appears at a point within range that you can see above yourself. It takes the shape of a Cylinder [Area of Effect] that is 10 feet tall with a 60-foot radius. When you cast the spell, choose a point you can see under the cloud. A lightning bolt shoots from the cloud to that point. Each creature within 5 feet of that point makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d10 Lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. Until the spell ends, you can take a Magic action on each of your turns to call down lightning again. Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot: the damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 3.

Cartographer Spells.spell.Clairvoyance.description: You create an Invisible sensor within range in a location familiar to you (a place you have visited or seen before) or in an obvious location that is unfamiliar to you (such as behind a door, around a corner, or in a grove of trees).

Cartographer Spells.spell.Banishment.description: One creature that you can see within range must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be transported to a harmless demiplane for the duration. While there, the target has the Incapacitated condition.

Cartographer Spells.spell.Locate Creatur.description: Describe or name a creature that is familiar to you. You sense the direction to the creature's location if that creature is within 1,000 feet of you. If the creature is moving, you know the direction of its movement.

Cartographer Spells.spell.Scrying.description: You can see and hear a creature you choose that is on the same plane of existence as you. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw, which is modified (see the tables below) by how well you know the target and the sort of physical connection you have to it.

Cartographer Spells.spell.Teleportation.description: As you cast the spell, you draw a 5-foot-radius circle on the ground inscribed with sigils that link your location to a permanent teleportation circle of your choice whose sigil sequence you know and that is on the same plane of existence as you.

Mapping Magic.description: You gain the following benefits. Illuminated Cartography: You can cast Faerie Fire without expending a spell slot, outlining the affected creatures as if in ink. You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Guided Precision.description: Once per turn, whenever you cast a spell from your Cartographer Spells list or hit a creature affected by your Faerie Fire with an attack roll, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of the spell or attack. In addition, taking damage can't cause you to lose Concentration on Faerie Fire.

Ingenious Movement.description: When you use your Flash of Genius, you or a willing creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of yourself can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see as part of that same Reaction.

Superior Atlas.description: Your Adventurer's Atlas improves, gaining the following benefits. Safe Haven: When a map holder would be reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, that creature can destroy its map. The creature's Hit Points instead change to a number equal to twice your Artificer level, and the creature is teleported to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you or another map holder of its choice.

Magic Item Tinker.field.Actions.option.M.description: Touch a replicated magic item with charges within 5 ft — expend a spell slot (level 1+) to restore charges equal to the slot level.

Rage.description: You can imbue yourself with a primal power called Rage, a force that grants you extraordinary might and resilience. You can enter it as a Bonus Action if you aren't wearing Heavy armor. You can enter your Rage the number of times shown for your Barbarian level in the Rages column of the Barbarian Features table. Your Rage lasts until the end of your next turn, and it ends early if you don Haven armor or have the Incapacitated condition. If your Rage would end and you have attacked a creature, forced a creature to make a saving throw, or taken a Bonus Action during this turn, your Rage instead continues until the end of your next turn. You can also extend your Rage for another round as a Bonus Action. It can last for up to 10 minutes.

Rage.field.Actions.option.Rage.description: Advantage on STR checks/saves, bonus melee damage, resistance to physical damage. Lasts up to 10 minutes

Weapon Mastery (Barbarian).description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the weapon mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial Melee weapons of your choice, such as Greataxe and Handaxe. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.

Danger Sense.description: You gain an uncanny sense of when things aren't as they should be, giving you an edge when you dodge perils. You have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws unless you have the Incapacitated condition.

Reckless Attack.description: You can throw aside all concern for defense to attack with increased ferocity. When you make your first attack roll on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you Advantage on attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn, but attack rolls against you have Advantage during that time.

Reckless Attack.field.Actions.option.Rec.description: Advantage on STR attack rolls this turn; attacks against you also have Advantage

Barbarian Subclass.description: You gain a Barbarian subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Barbarian levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Barbarian level or lower.

Primal Knowledge.description: You gain proficiency in another skill of your choice from the skill list available to Barbarians at level 1. In addition, while your Rage is active, you can channel primal power when you attempt certain tasks; whenever you make an ability check using one of the following skills, you can make it as a Strength check even if it normally uses a different ability: Acrobatics, Intimidation, Perception, Stealth, or Survival. When you use this ability, your Strength represents primal power coursing through you, honing your agility, bearing, and senses.

Fast Movement.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor.

Feral Instinct.description: Your instincts are so honed that you have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

Instinctive Pounce.description: As part of the Bonus Action you take to enter your Rage, you can move up to half your Speed.

Brutal Strike.description: If you use Reckless Attack, you can forgo any Advantage on one Strength-based attack roll of your choice on your turn. The chosen attack roll mustn't have Disadvantage. If the chosen attack roll hits, the target takes an extra 1d10 damage of the same type dealt by the weapon or Unarmed Strike, and you can cause one Brutal Strike effect of your choice. You have the following effect options. Forceful Blow. The target is pushed 15 feet straight away from you. You can then move up to 15 feet straight toward the target without provoking Opportunity Attacks. Hamstring Blow. The target's Speed is reduced by 15 feet until the start of your next turn. A target can be affected by only one Hamstring Blow at a time.

Brutal Strike.field.Actions.option.Force.description: Push target 15 ft away, then move 15 ft toward it without provoking OAs

Brutal Strike.field.Actions.option.Hamst.description: Target's Speed reduced by 15 ft until start of your next turn

Brutal Strike.field.Actions.option.Stagg.description: Target has Disadvantage on next save and can't make OAs until start of your next turn

Brutal Strike.field.Actions.option.Sunde.description: Target's AC reduced by Rage Damage bonus until start of your next turn

Relentless Rage.description: Your Rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 Hit Points while your Rage is active and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, your Hit Points instead change to a number equal to twice your Barbarian level. Each time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5.

Improved Brutal Strike.description: You have honed new ways to attack furiously. The following effects are now among your Brutal Strike options. Staggering Blow. The target has Disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes, and it can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of your next turn. Sundering Blow. Before the first damage roll for the Brutal Strike, the target's AC is reduced by an amount equal to your Rage Damage bonus. This reduction lasts until the start of your next turn, and a target can be affected by only one Sundering Blow at a time.

Improved Brutal Strike 2.description: Your Brutal Strike damage increases to 2d10. In addition, you can use two different Brutal Strike effects whenever you use your Brutal Strike.

Persistent Rage.description: When you roll Initiative, you can regain all expended uses of Rage. After you regain uses of Rage in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. In addition, your Rage is so fierce that it now lasts for 10 minutes without you needing to do anything to extend it from round to round.

Indomitable Might.description: If your total for a Strength check or Strength saving throw is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

Primal Champion.description: You embody primal power. Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

Frenzy.description: If you use Reckless Attack while your Rage is active, you deal extra damage to the first target you hit on your turn with a Strength-based attack. To determine the extra damage, roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. The damage has the same type as the weapon or Unarmed Strike used for the attack.

Mindless Rage.description: You have Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened conditions while your Rage is active. If you're Charmed or Frightened when you enter your Rage, the condition ends on you.

Retaliation.description: When you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to make one melee attack against that creature, using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike.

Intimidating Presence.description: As a Bonus Action, you can strike terror into others with your menacing presence and primal power. When you do so, each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation [Area of Effect] originating from you must make a Wisdom saving throw (8 plus your Strength modifier and Proficiency). On a failed save, a creature has the Frightened condition for 1 minute.

Battlerager Armor.description: You gain the ability to use spiked armor as a weapon. While you are wearing spiked armor and are raging, you can use a bonus action to make one melee weapon attack with your armor spikes at a target within 5 feet of you. If the attack hits, the spikes deal 1d4 piercing damage. You use your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls.

Restriction—Dwarves Only.description: Only dwarves can follow the Path of the Battlerager. The battlerager fills a particular niche in dwarven society and culture. Your DM can lift this restriction to better suit the campaign. The restriction exists for the Forgotten Realms. It might not apply to your DM's setting or your DM's version of the Realms.

Reckless Abandon.description: When you use Reckless Attack while raging, you also gain temporary hit points equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1). They vanish if any of them are left when your rage ends.

Battlerager Charge.description: You can take the Dash action as a bonus action while you are raging.

Spiked Retribution.description: When a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack, the attacker takes 3 piercing damage if you are raging, aren't incapacitated, and are wearing spiked armor.

Ancestral Protectors.description: Spectral warriors appear when you enter your rage. While you're raging, the first creature you hit with an attack on your turn becomes the target of the warriors, which hinder its attacks.

Spirit Shield.description: The guardian spirits that aid you can provide supernatural protection to those you defend. If you are raging and another creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your reaction to reduce that damage by 2d6. When you reach certain levels in this class, you can reduce the damage by more: by 3d6 at 10th level and by 4d6 at 14th level.

Consult the Spirits.description: You gain the ability to consult with your ancestral spirits. When you do so, you cast the augury or clairvoyance spell, without using a spell slot or material components. Rather than creating a spherical sensor, this use of clairvoyance invisibly summons one of your ancestral spirits to the chosen location. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Vengeful Ancestors.description: Your ancestral spirits grow powerful enough to retaliate. When you use your Spirit Shield to reduce the damage of an attack, the attacker takes an amount of force damage equal to the damage that your Spirit Shield prevents.

Desert.description: When this effect is activated, all other creatures in your aura take 2 fire damage each. The damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 3 at 5th level, 4 at 10th level, 5 at 15th level, and 6 at 20th level.

Sea.description: When this effect is activated, you can choose one other creature you can see in your aura. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. The target takes 1d6 lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 2d6 at 10th level, 3d6 at 15th level, and 4d6 at 20th level.

Tundra.description: When this effect is activated, each creature of your choice in your aura gains 2 temporary hit points, as icy spirits inure it to suffering. The temporary hit points increase when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 3 at 5th level, 4 at 10th level, 5 at 15th level, and 6 at 20th level.

Storm Aura.description: You emanate a stormy, magical aura while you rage. The aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction, but not through Cover. Your aura has an effect that activates when you enter your rage, and you can activate the effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action. Choose desert, sea, or tundra. Your aura's effect depends on that chosen environment, as detailed below.

Storm Soul.description: The storm grants you benefits even when your aura isn't active. The benefits are based on the environment you chose for your Storm Aura.
